etymology.description: Depicts something carved/engraved -> proto-form of 㓞(㓞) -> variant of 王(玉)
etymology.interpretation: The character 丯(丯) originally depicted something that was carved or engraved, serving as a proto-form of 㓞(㓞), which means 'cut'. It was later used as a variant of 玉(玉) (yù, 'jade'), distinguished by adding 王(王) (wáng, 'king') to symbolize a king's authority.
